about.title: About ROOTS
about.description: Developed within the scope of the ATT (Accelerate and Transform Tourism) project of the Recovery and Resilience Plan, ROOTS is a tourism data monitoring platform for decision support in Barra and Costa Nova.
about.objective_description: Simplify data analysis and make decision-making more informed. We transform scattered information into useful knowledge about local tourism. Official data, organized and available in a click: visualize, compare, export. If you make decisions about the territory, this platform is for you.

footer.tagline_1: The Territory is Built by Many Hands.

quem_somos.title: Who are we?
quem_somos.intro_p1: Born in 2026, the ROOTS platform (Research Observatory for Optimising Tourism Sustainability) is an observatory for monitoring tourism activity in the municipality of Ílhavo.
quem_somos.intro_p2: Developed by the University of Aveiro under the ATT3 project, part of the ATT Mobilising Agenda (Accelerate and Transform Tourism), ROOTS aims to support strategic planning and local tourism management through the collection, processing, and dissemination of relevant information for various sector agents. It contributes to more informed, sustainable, and competitive destination management, supporting public entities, businesses, and the local community through indicator monitoring and tourism dynamics analysis.
quem_somos.missao_desc: To promote knowledge of tourism activity in Ílhavo through the collection, analysis, and dissemination of information that contributes to the sustainable development of the destination.
quem_somos.visao_desc: To establish ROOTS as a reference in the production and sharing of knowledge about local tourism, contributing to a more sustainable, innovative, and competitive tourist destination.
quem_somos.obj_monitorizar_desc: The evolution of tourism activity in the territory.
quem_somos.obj_produzir_desc: Indicators and studies to support strategic tourism planning.
quem_somos.obj_disponibilizar_desc: Relevant information for public decision-makers, businesses, and the community.
quem_somos.obj_reforcar_desc: Knowledge about visitors, residents, and sector agents.

governanca.title: Governance
governanca.intro: ROOTS governance is based on a collaborative structure involving public entities, academic institutions, and tourism sector agents. This model seeks to ensure scientific rigor, institutional coordination, and connection to the territory, allowing tourism monitoring to be carried out in an integrated and participatory manner.

territorio.area_p1: ROOTS monitors tourism activity in the territory of the municipality of Ílhavo, located in the Aveiro Region, within the Central Region.
territorio.area_p2: The municipality holds a strategic position on Portugal's central coastal strip, integrating the Ria de Aveiro lagoon system and establishing a strong relationship between the coastal environment, natural heritage, and economic activities associated with the sea. The municipality includes urban areas, coastal zones, and natural spaces associated with the Ria, combining different landscapes and resources that contribute to the diversity of the tourist experience.
territorio.caract_desc: The municipality holds a strategic position on Portugal's central coastal strip, integrating the Ria de Aveiro lagoon system and establishing a strong relationship between the coastal environment, natural heritage, and economic activities associated with the sea. The municipality includes urban areas, coastal zones, and natural spaces associated with the Ria, combining different landscapes and resources that contribute to the diversity of the tourist experience.
territorio.recursos_desc: Ílhavo has a strong maritime and cultural identity, historically linked to cod fishing and port activity, elements that continue to shape the territory's identity and tourism offering.

redes.conhecimento_p1: ROOTS seeks to integrate knowledge-sharing networks related to sustainable tourism monitoring.
redes.iniciativas_intro: The Observatory follows and promotes initiatives related to sustainability and innovation in tourism, including:
